data.title: TREMFYA® approved for subcutaneous induction in adults with UC, first IL-23 inhibitor with fully subcutaneous regimen.
data.description: TREMFYA® now allows patients to self-administer their treatment from the beginning, in addition to its previous approval for subcutaneous induction in Crohn's disease. In a study, TREMFYA® showed notable results with high rates of clinical remission and endoscopic improvement compared to a placebo at Week 12.

data.title: Dow Jones rises after Fed cuts interest rates; Nvidia stock falls. Nasdaq lags. Powell cites 'challenging situation'.
data.description: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones index experienced an increase following the Federal Reserve's decision to reduce interest rates. Conversely, Nvidia stock faced a decline. The stock market witnessed the Dow Jones jumping, while the Nasdaq lagged behind. Federal Reserve Chairman Powell acknowledged a 'challenging situation' during the event. This information was provided by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: RedCloud, Nasdaq: RCT, presenting AI-Native vision at Roth AI Summit on Sept 24 to transform global trade.
data.description: In London on September 19, 2025, RedCloud Holdings plc (Nasdaq: RCT) disclosed that their CEO and Co-Founder, Justin Floyd, is set to speak at the 3rd Annual Roth AI Summit 2025 on September 24. The company, known for the RedAI trading platform utilized by Fast Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG) Manufacturers, Distributors, and Retailers, shared this announcement. It signifies a significant opportunity for stakeholders to gain insights from a prominent figure in the industry and highlights the importance of RedCloud’s contributions to the technology sector. The event is set to take place soon, marking a key date for those interested in the advancements within this space.

data.title: Tech stocks surge as Fed cuts rates by 25 basis points; Nasdaq 100 recovers to 24,200 points. Trade tensions impact AI industry. Fed funds target drops to 4.00%-4.25%. Trump pressures for lower rates. Fed Governor dissents, suggests 50-basis-point cut.
data.description: Tech stocks surged on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve announced a 25-basis-point rate cut and hinted at further easing due to a weakening labor market and downside employment risks. The Nasdaq 100, tracked by the Invesco QQQ Trust, rebounded and ended positively at the 24,200-point level after dipping earlier. AI-related stocks like Nvidia Corp., Broadcom Inc., and Palantir Technologies Inc. dropped up to 3% post-China's ban on Nvidia's AI chip purchases. The rate cut brings the federal funds target to 4.00%-4.25%, the fourth in the easing cycle and the Fed's first move after a nine-month hiatus. This decision follows persistent calls from President Trump for lower rates to boost consumption and investment. Although not unanimous, with Fed Governor Stephen Miran dissenting in favor of a 50-basis-point cut, the tech sector saw a significant uptick, with details available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Kentucky First Federal Bancorp reports Q2 net income of $176,000, an increase of $1.3 million from last year. Total assets at $371.2 million.
data.description: Kentucky First Federal Bancorp reported a significant increase in net income for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, amounting to $176,000, compared to a loss of $1.1 million in the same period in the previous year. Total assets as of June 30, 2025, decreased by 1.0% to $371.2 million, primarily due to a decrease in loans and an increase in cash and cash equivalents. The Company's book value per share was reported as $5.98, and shareholders' equity increased to $48.4 million. Net interest income increased by 21.1% to $2.3 million, with interest income rising to $5.0 million and interest expense increasing to $2.7 million. Additionally, non-interest income rose by 113.5% to $111,000, mostly due to an increase in net gains on sales of loans. Total liabilities decreased to $322.8 million, with FHLB advances decreasing significantly. Overall, the Company showed positive financial growth and performance, driven by an improved interest rate environment and increased demand for fixed-rate secondary market loans.

data.title: Fed Chief Powell hints at more rate cuts but warns of uncertainty. Stay tuned for updates.
data.description: Jerome Powell, the Chair of the Federal Reserve, is currently addressing the public. Following the Federal Reserve's recent actions, there are indications of potential further reductions in interest rates. However, Powell expressed caution, advising against absolute certainty regarding future rate cuts. Stay updated with the live coverage for more details.
